intro: Chrysophyllum boukokoense, also known as Bukoko, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysophyllum boukokoense include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysophyllum boukokoense is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as teas, tonics, and poultices, to leverage its health benefits.
